query_used: Very Big Reasoning Model video reasoning VBVR
findings: ResearchFinding(title='Direct Link 1', url='https://huggingface.co/papers/2602.20159', snippet='Content provided directly by user.', full_content='Abstract\nA large-scale video reasoning dataset and benchmark are introduced to study video intelligence capabilities beyond visual quality, enabling systematic analysis of spatiotemporal reasoning and generalization across diverse tasks.\nRapid progress in video models has largely focused on visual quality, leaving their reasoning capabilities underexplored. Video reasoning grounds intelligence in spatiotemporally consistent visual environments that go beyond what text can naturally capture, enabling intuitive reasoning over spatiotemporal structure such as continuity, interaction, and causality. However, systematically studying video reasoning and its scaling behavior is hindered by the lack of large-scale training data. To address this gap, we introduce the Very Big Video Reasoning (VBVR) Dataset, an unprecedentedly large-scale resource spanning 200 curated reasoning tasks following a principled taxonomy and over one million video clips, approximately three orders of magnitude larger than existing datasets. We further present VBVR-Bench, a verifiable evaluation framework that moves beyond model-based judging by incorporating rule-based, human-aligned scorers, enabling reproducible and interpretable diagnosis of video reasoning capabilities. Leveraging the VBVR suite, we conduct one of the first large-scale scaling studies of video reasoning and observe early signs of emergent generalization to unseen reasoning tasks. Together, VBVR lays a foundation for the next stage of research in generalizable video reasoning. The data, benchmark toolkit, and models are publicly available at https://video-reason.com/ .', relevance_score=1.0)

ResearchFinding(title='Direct Link 3', url='https://huggingface.co/Video-Reason/VBVR-Wan2.2', snippet='Content provided directly by user.', full_content='VBVR: A Very Big Video Reasoning Suite
Overview
Video reasoning grounds intelligence in spatiotemporally consistent visual environments that go beyond what text can naturally capture, enabling intuitive reasoning over motion, interaction, and causality. Rapid progress in video models has focused primarily on visual quality. Systematically studying video reasoning and its scaling behavior suffers from a lack of video reasoning (training) data. To address this gap, we introduce the Very Big Video Reasoning (VBVR) Dataset, an unprecedentedly large-scale resource spanning 200 curated reasoning tasks and over one million video clips—approximately three orders of magnitude larger than existing datasets. We further present VBVR-Bench, a verifiable evaluation framework that moves beyond model-based judging by incorporating rule-based, human-aligned scorers, enabling reproducible and interpretable diagnosis of video reasoning capabilities. Leveraging the VBVR suite, we conduct one of the first large-scale scaling studies of video reasoning and observe early signs of emergent generalization to unseen reasoning tasks. Together, VBVR lays a foundation for the next stage of research in generalizable video reasoning.
Release Information
VBVR-Wan2.2 is trained from Wan2.2-I2V-A14B without architectural modifications, as the goal of VBVR-Wan2.2 is to investigate data scaling behavior and provide a strong baseline model for the video reasoning research community. Leveraging the VBVR-Dataset, which to our knowledge constitutes one of the largest video reasoning datasets to date, VBVR-Wan2.2 achieved highest score on VBVR-Bench.
In this release, we present VBVR-Wan2.2, VBVR-Dataset, VBVR-Bench-Data and VBVR-Bench-Leaderboard.
🛠️ QuickStart
Installation
We recommend using uv to manage the environment.
uv installation guide: https://docs.astral.sh/uv/getting-started/installation/#installing-uv
pip install torch>=2.4.0 torchvision>=0.19.0 transformers Pillow huggingface_hub[cli]
uv pip install git+https://github.com/huggingface/diffusers
Example Code
huggingface-cli download Video-Reason/VBVR-Wan2.2 --local-dir ./VBVR-Wan2.2
python example.py \
--model_path ./VBVR-Wan2.2

ResearchFinding(title='Direct Link 4', url='https://huggingface.co/datasets/Video-Reason/VBVR-Dataset', snippet='Content provided directly by user.', full_content='datasets
🌐 Website • 📊 VBVR-Bench • 🏆 Leaderboard
VBVR-Dataset is an unprecedentedly large-scale video reasoning training resource, part of the Very Big Video Reasoning (VBVR) Suite. This release contains the training split: 100 curated reasoning task generators with 1,000,000 video clips (10,000 samples per generator), with each sample consisting of a video, start/end frames, a textual reasoning prompt, and structured metadata.
This dataset is designed to support large-scale training and scaling studies of video reasoning capabilities in multimodal AI models.
The data/metadata.parquet file contains 1,000,000 rows with the following columns, viewable directly in the HF Dataset Viewer:
data/metadata.parquet
id
generator
G-11_handle_object_reappearance_data-generator
task
sample_id
handle_object_reappearance_00000000
prompt
metadata_json
first_frame_path
final_frame_path
ground_truth_video_path
tar_file
tars/G-11_handle_object_reappearance_data-generator.tar
The actual video/image data is stored as 100 individual tar files in the tars/ directory, one per generator. Each tar contains the full directory structure:
tars/
<generator_name>/ <task_name>/ <sample_id>/ first_frame.png # Initial frame of the video final_frame.png # Final frame of the video ground_truth.mp4 # Full video sequence (ground truth) metadata.json # Structured generation metadata prompt.txt # Textual reasoning prompt
These tasks test spatial reasoning, graph navigation, pattern recognition, and geometric understanding:
These tasks test physical reasoning, transformation understanding, and causal inference:
The metadata is directly viewable in the Dataset Viewer tab above. You can explore prompts, task types, and sample distributions without downloading anything.
from datasets import load_dataset ds = load_dataset("Video-Reason/VBVR-Dataset", split="train") print(f"Total samples: {len(ds)}") print(ds[0]) # View first sample metadata
from huggingface_hub import hf_hub_download # Download a specific generator's tar tar_path = hf_hub_download( repo_id="Video-Reason/VBVR-Dataset", filename="tars/G-11_handle_object_reappearance_data-generator.tar", repo_type="dataset", ) # Extract import tarfile with tarfile.open(tar_path) as tar: tar.extractall("./data")
# Using huggingface-cli huggingface-cli download Video-Reason/VBVR-Dataset --include "tars/*.tar" --repo-type dataset --local-dir ./vbvr-data # Or using git lfs GIT_LFS_SKIP_SMUDGE=1 git clone https://huggingface.co/datasets/Video-Reason/VBVR-Dataset cd VBVR-Dataset git lfs pull --include "tars/*.tar"

key_facts: VBVR stands for Very Big Video Reasoning, a large-scale video reasoning dataset and benchmark
The dataset contains over 1 million video clips spanning 200 curated reasoning tasks
VBVR is approximately three orders of magnitude larger than existing video reasoning datasets
VBVR-Bench provides a verifiable evaluation framework with rule-based, human-aligned scorers for reproducible assessment
The dataset includes 100 task generators with 10,000 samples each (1,000,000 total samples)
Each sample contains a video, start/end frames, textual reasoning prompt, and structured metadata
VBVR-Wan2.2 model is trained from Wan2.2-I2V-A14B without architectural modifications
The research focuses on investigating data scaling behavior in video reasoning
Tasks cover spatial reasoning, graph navigation, pattern recognition, and geometric understanding
The benchmark enables systematic analysis of spatiotemporal reasoning and generalization across diverse tasks
Early signs of emergent generalization to unseen reasoning tasks were observed in scaling studies
The dataset moves beyond visual quality to focus on reasoning capabilities in video models
Video reasoning grounds intelligence in spatiotemporally consistent visual environments
The structured metadata includes generator name, task name, sample ID, prompt, and paths to video/image data
structured_summary: VBVR (Very Big Video Reasoning) is an unprecedentedly large-scale video reasoning dataset and benchmark designed to study video intelligence capabilities beyond visual quality. It contains over 1 million video clips across 200 curated reasoning tasks, making it approximately three orders of magnitude larger than existing datasets. The suite includes VBVR-Dataset (training data with 100 task generators) and VBVR-Bench (evaluation framework with rule-based scorers). VBVR-Wan2.2 is a baseline model trained from Wan2.2-I2V-A14B to investigate data scaling behavior. The research enables systematic analysis of spatiotemporal reasoning including spatial reasoning, graph navigation, pattern recognition, and geometric understanding. Early signs of emergent generalization to unseen reasoning tasks have been observed, laying a foundation for generalizable video reasoning research.
